content: Somebody mentioned patchwork here at some point - would it be possible for Rust to get a page? I assume it would just be a request to patchwork@lists.ozlabs.org
It doesn't seem extremely useful, but a low effort way to have a quick overview of everything going on does seem nice

Yeah, we have been discussing lately how to give a better overview of everything that is going on. Patches are a big part of it, and we are looking into patchwork as an improvement for that as well as other possibilities. Nevertheless, subscribing to the mailing list is recommended! Perhaps with a filter to put it into a tag/folder and then reading it from time to time -- it is not very high traffic so far.

There are also quite a few other things going on usually (i.e. apart from the patches), and it would be nice to cover some more of that (at least at a high-level), if possible, at https://rust-for-linux.com.

We also have the "open meeting" back now again, which we encourage everybody to join since it is a great way to know more about what is going on! If there is enough demand for it, we could increase its frequency too.

I've been reading everything on the list for months but only recently figured out how to subscribe so I could eventually say things, lol. Is there much overhead to enabling our list to patchwork? If something specific is needed, I could figure it out after I wrap up the clippy thing

I don't think it would be hard to enable (and I think someone from the team was taking a look already to see what would be required). It is more of a matter of deciding whether we will use it or not and to what degree (e.g. it may be useful even if we don't keep state there apart from what the system automatically figures out from the mailing list).

Trevor Gross said:

I've been reading everything on the list for months but only recently figured out how to subscribe so I could eventually say things, lol. Is there much overhead to enabling our list to patchwork? If something specific is needed, I could figure it out after I wrap up the clippy thing

well, you could use b4 mbox to download an email, and use your favorite MUA to reply. I.e. you don't need to subscribe the list to reply.

Miguel Ojeda said:

I don't think it would be hard to enable (and I think someone from the team was taking a look already to see what would be required). It is more of a matter of deciding whether we will use it or not and to what degree (e.g. it may be useful even if we don't keep state there apart from what the system automatically figures out from the mailing list).

That's all I was thinking, just the R count gives a good quick idea of how far along it is

Boqun Feng said:

Trevor Gross said:
well, you could use b4 mbox to download an email, and use your favorite MUA to reply. I.e. you don't need to subscribe the list to reply.

I also just don't have much to say :smile: but I appreciate the tip

Boqun Feng said:

well, you could use b4 mbox to download an email, and use your favorite MUA to reply. I.e. you don't need to subscribe the list to reply.

Yeah, Lore also gives generated instructions to reply to particular emails with just git send-email -- look for Reply instructions: at the bottom of the "permalink" version of emails, e.g. https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/CANiq72k+uB4Px=_kGNEiduc0AewC05XO2zTqC3+ABa0hcxui9w@mail.gmail.com/#R
